title: Ride Sharing Dispatch System
summary: A hybrid C++ plus Next.js ride dispatch platform that manages cities, drivers, riders, and trip state through API-driven workflows.
problem: Dispatch simulation in pure console code made it hard to demo real-world ride flow, API behavior, and state transitions to non-technical stakeholders.
approach: Designed a C++ backend that models dispatch rules, rider-driver matching, and trip lifecycle management.
Added HTTP endpoints for locations, drivers, trips, trip actions, and rollback operations.
Built a Next.js frontend mode that can run with built-in API routes for deployment and demos.
impact: Enabled end-to-end demos of dispatch logic without requiring a local-only setup.
Improved visibility into operational state by exposing trip and driver data through API routes.
Made the system presentation-ready for portfolio and client walkthroughs.
features: Trip request, start, complete, and cancel flows
Deployable Next.js interface with API proxy mode

title: Supply Chain Management System
summary: A C++ OOP supply-chain system with a premium web dashboard for inventory, order lifecycle tracking, and smart low-stock and expiry insights.
problem: Operations teams needed a clear way to manage stock, perishable risk, and pending fulfillment while still preserving robust C++ core business logic.
approach: Implemented the core inventory and order engine in C++ with file persistence and object-oriented abstractions.
Built a client-ready frontend dashboard with login, KPI modules, and report-friendly views.
Added demo data seeding, export/import workflows, and print mode for stakeholder presentations.
impact: Improved decision speed with dashboard KPIs for stock, pending orders, and expiry risk.
Made enterprise demos smoother through responsive UI and guided presentation flow.
Connected academic OOP depth with practical product-style delivery.
features: Smart insights, reporting, and data export/import
